message:
chore(artifacts,beads): refresh verification evidence for bd-m8p (171 checks, all passing) and update operator E2E summary and issue tracker

- artifacts/section_10_9/bd-m8p: verification evidence now covers 171
  checks (up from 152) including all 10 capsule reason codes, the
  CapsuleVerificationFailure type, and 6 new capsule verification tests
- artifacts/replacement_gap/bd-1z5a: operator E2E summary table
  simplified to remove per-row provenance columns (build_id_kind,
  worker, completed, duration) that are now tracked in the JSON summary
- .beads/issues.jsonl: updated issue tracker state

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-1gx","title":"[10.4] Define signed extension package manifest schema.","description":"Master Plan Source: PLAN_TO_CREATE_FRANKEN_NODE.md
Section: 10.4 — Extension Ecosystem + Registry (Item 1 of 8)

Why This Exists:
The signed extension package manifest schema is the foundation for the entire extension trust ecosystem. Every extension must have a machine-readable, cryptographically signed manifest that declares its capabilities, provenance, and behavioral profile. This is the first step in building the trust-native extension distribution network.

Task Objective:
Define the canonical schema for signed extension package manifests that will be used across the registry, trust card system, and policy evaluation pipeline.

Detailed Acceptance Criteria:
1. Schema defines required fields: package identity, version, author/publisher, capability declarations (FsRead, FsWrite, NetworkEgress, ProcessSpawn, EnvRead per franken_engine ExtensionManifest), behavioral profile, minimum runtime version.
2. Schema includes provenance fields: build system, source repository, reproducibility markers, attestation chain.
3. Schema includes trust metadata: certification level, revocation status pointer, trust card reference.
4. Manifest is cryptographically signed with publisher key; signature scheme supports threshold signing for high-impact operations (9B.7).
5. Schema versioning strategy with backward-compatible evolution path.
6. Schema is published as both human-readable spec and JSON Schema/machine-readable format.
7. Integration with franken_engine ExtensionManifest validation (from frankenengine-extension-host crate).

Key Dependencies:
- Depends on 10.1 (Charter) for governance boundaries on what extensions can declare.
- Depends on 10.N (Normalization) for canonical ownership rules.
- Consumed by 10.4 remaining items (provenance, trust cards, registry).
- Consumed by 10.13 (FCP) for manifest negotiation and admission.

Expected Artifacts:
- Schema definition document at docs/specs/section_10_4/extension_manifest_schema.md
- JSON Schema at schemas/extension_manifest.schema.json
- Rust types in src/supply_chain/manifest.rs implementing the schema
- artifacts/section_10_4/bd-1gx/verification_evidence.json

Testing and Logging Requirements:
- Unit tests: schema validation (valid manifests, invalid manifests, missing required fields, malformed signatures).
- Integration tests: manifest creation -> signing -> validation -> registry admission pipeline.
- Fuzz tests: malformed manifest payloads.
- Structured logs: MANIFEST_CREATED, MANIFEST_SIGNED, MANIFEST_VALIDATED, MANIFEST_REJECTED with rejection reason codes.","notes":"Plan-space QA addendum (2026-02-20):
1) Preserve full canonical-plan scope; no feature compression or silent omission is allowed.
2) Completion requires comprehensive verification coverage appropriate to scope: unit tests, integration tests, and E2E scripts/workflows with detailed structured logging (stable event/error codes + trace correlation IDs).
3) Completion requires reproducible evidence artifacts (machine-readable + human-readable) that allow independent replay and root-cause triage.
4) Any implementation PR for this bead must include explicit links to the above test/logging artifacts.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"WhiteMoose","created_at":"2026-02-20T07:36:45.428676958Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-20T17:21:42.678276477Z","closed_at":"2026-02-20T17:21:42.678250068Z","close_reason":"Implemented signed extension manifest schema, Rust validation module, and verification artifacts","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["plan","section-10-4","self-contained","test-obligations"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1gx","depends_on_id":"bd-1ta","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-20T07:46:36.052669446Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1gx","depends_on_id":"bd-3il","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-20T07:46:36.098543578Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1gx","depends_on_id":"bd-cda","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-20T07:46:36.144056928Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

-{"id":"bd-1h2w4","title":"[bd-1z5a support] Add stable replay-capsule reason codes in verifier_economy","description":"Why this exists: crates/franken-node/src/verifier_economy/mod.rs currently collapses replay capsule rejection to generic ERR_VEP_INVALID_CAPSULE without a stable subreason, which weakens operator-facing diagnostics for the active bd-1z5a canonical signed-capsule verifier lane. What this task must do: add deterministic replay-capsule verification reason codes / detailed failure surface in verifier_economy, use it in register/access rejection paths, add focused inline regressions, and extend the verifier-economy checker/tests so the reason-coded surface fails closed if removed. Acceptance criteria: 1. replay capsule verification exposes stable failure reasons for schema, freshness, attestation binding, trace commitment, integrity hash, and signature failures; 2. register/access rejection surfaces include those reason codes deterministically; 3. inline Rust tests plus checker/tests cover representative failure reasons; 4. cargo-heavy validation uses rch only.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-12T11:30:20.499256267Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-12T11:30:52.260965679Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

What this task must do: add deterministic replay-capsule verification reason codes / detailed failure surface in verifier_economy, use it in register/access rejection paths, add focused inline regressions, and extend the verifier-economy checker/tests so the reason-coded surface fails closed if removed. Acceptance criteria: 1. replay capsule verification exposes stable failure reasons for schema, freshness, attestation binding, trace commitment, integrity hash, and signature failures; 2. register/access rejection surfaces include those reason codes deterministically; 3. inline Rust tests plus checker/tests cover representative failure reasons; 4. cargo-heavy validation uses rch only.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"CrimsonCrane","created_at":"2026-03-12T11:30:20.499256267Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-12T11:31:35.418787327Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-1h6","title":"[10.13] Implement standard connector method contract validator (`handshake/describe/introspect/capabilities/configure/simulate/invoke/health/shutdown`).","description":"Master Plan Source: PLAN_TO_CREATE_FRANKEN_NODE.md
Section: 10.13 — FCP Deep-Mined Expansion Execution Track (9I)

Why This Exists:
Deep connector/provider contract track: lifecycle FSMs, conformance harnesses, fencing, sandboxing, revocation freshness, and protocol hardening.

Task Objective:
Implement standard connector method contract validator (`handshake/describe/introspect/capabilities/configure/simulate/invoke/health/shutdown`).

Acceptance Criteria:
- Validator rejects missing or schema-invalid methods; method schemas are versioned and pinned; contract report is machine-readable.

Expected Artifacts:
- `src/conformance/connector_method_validator.rs`, `docs/specs/connector_method_contract.md`, `artifacts/10.13/connector_method_contract_report.json`.

- Machine-readable verification artifact at `artifacts/section_10_13/bd-1h6/verification_evidence.json` for CI/release gating.
- Human-readable verification summary at `artifacts/section_10_13/bd-1h6/verification_summary.md` linking implementation intent to measured outcomes.
Testing & Logging Requirements:
- Unit tests for core logic, invariants, and error handling.
- Integration/E2E tests for end-to-end control-flow behavior.
- Detailed structured logs/telemetry with stable codes and trace correlation IDs.
- Deterministic replay fixture or reproducible artifact for failure analysis.

Task-Specific Clarification:
- For "[10.13] Implement standard connector method contract validator (`handshake/describe/introspect/capabilities/configure/simulate/invoke/health/shutdown`).", preserve full capability scope from the plan; no feature compression or silent omission is allowed.
- Verification for "[10.13] Implement standard connector method contract validator (`handshake/describe/introspect/capabilities/configure/simulate/invoke/health/shutdown`)." must include capability-specific thresholds and machine-readable pass/fail evidence artifacts.
- Unit tests, integration/E2E scripts, and structured logs for "[10.13] Implement standard connector method contract validator (`handshake/describe/introspect/capabilities/configure/simulate/invoke/health/shutdown`)." must support deterministic replay and root-cause triage without hidden context.

Why This Improves User Outcomes:
- "[10.13] Implement standard connector method contract validator (`handshake/describe/introspect/capabilities/configure/simulate/invoke/health/shutdown`)." is required to reduce operator error, increase deterministic safety guarantees, and improve time-to-diagnosis for failures in real-world workflows.
- Explicit success/failure evidence for "[10.13] Implement standard connector method contract validator (`handshake/describe/introspect/capabilities/configure/simulate/invoke/health/shutdown`)." prevents false confidence and improves trust in migration/compatibility/security outcomes.
- Detailed unit + integration/E2E + structured logging requirements ensure reproducible quality and faster incident remediation for users.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"CrimsonCrane","created_at":"2026-02-20T07:36:51.557602070Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-20T10:40:22.765101588Z","closed_at":"2026-02-20T10:40:22.765075921Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["plan","section-10-13","self-contained","test-obligations"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1h6","depends_on_id":"bd-1rk","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-20T07:43:12.315822708Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"bd-1w78","title":"[13] Success criterion: continuous lockstep validation","description":"Master Plan Source: PLAN_TO_CREATE_FRANKEN_NODE.md
Section: 13

Task Objective:
Instrument and enforce continuous lockstep validation for compatibility claims.

Execution Requirements:
- Make this requirement machine-verifiable and release-gated where applicable.
- Keep behavior self-documenting so future contributors do not need to re-open the master plan.

Testing & Logging Requirements:
- Unit tests for rule/contract logic and error conditions.
- Integration/E2E tests for workflow-level enforcement.
- Structured logs with stable codes and trace IDs.
- Reproducible artifacts that prove contract satisfaction.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Success and failure invariants for [13] Success criterion: continuous lockstep validation are explicitly quantified and machine-verifiable.
- Determinism requirements for [13] Success criterion: continuous lockstep validation are validated across repeated runs and adversarial perturbations.


Expected Artifacts:
- Machine-readable verification artifact with explicit canonical path under artifacts/section_13/bd-1w78/verification_evidence.json, suitable for CI/release gating.
- Human-readable verification summary with explicit canonical path under artifacts/section_13/bd-1w78/verification_summary.md, linking implementation intent to measured validation outcomes.

Task-Specific Clarification:
- The implementation must deliver the exact capability named in the title, with no scope dilution and no silent feature omission.
- Validation artifacts must include capability-specific thresholds, pass/fail criteria, and reproducible evidence bundles tied to this bead ID.
- Program/section verification gates must be able to consume this bead's outputs without manual interpretation.

Why This Improves User Outcomes:
- "[13] Success criterion: continuous lockstep validation" is required to reduce operator error, increase deterministic safety guarantees, and improve time-to-diagnosis for failures in real-world workflows.
- Explicit success/failure evidence for "[13] Success criterion: continuous lockstep validation" prevents false confidence and improves trust in migration/compatibility/security outcomes.
- Detailed unit + integration/E2E + structured logging requirements ensure reproducible quality and faster incident remediation for users.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Lockstep validation runs continuously: every CI pipeline execution includes a lockstep comparison against reference Node.js on a representative test subset.
2. The representative subset covers >= 100 API calls across critical API families (fs, http, crypto, stream, net).
3. Any lockstep divergence fails CI and produces a divergence receipt with: input, expected output, actual output, API family, severity.
4. Lockstep validation history is tracked: a dashboard or report shows pass/fail trend over the last 30 days.
5. Divergence receipts are immutable once created (append-only storage).
6. Lockstep validation can be triggered on-demand for a specific API family (not just full suite).
7. Evidence: lockstep_validation_status.json with latest run date, pass rate, and list of any open divergences.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"CrimsonCrane","created_at":"2026-02-20T07:39:34.389711289Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-20T23:10:09.598558027Z","closed_at":"2026-02-20T23:10:09.598504697Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["plan","section-13","self-contained","test-obligations"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1w78","depends_on_id":"bd-2f43","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-20T07:43:25.313179869Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"bd-1wpq","title":"Fix certification registry stale evaluation after promote/demote","description":"Fresh-eyes audit found that crates/franken-node/src/supply_chain/certification.rs updates CertificationRecord.level during promote/demote but leaves CertificationRecord.evaluation stale. Downstream readers can observe conflicting certification levels from the same record. Fix the registry to keep evaluation metadata consistent with promoted/demoted state and add focused regressions.","notes":"Patched crates/franken-node/src/supply_chain/certification.rs so CertificationRegistry::promote() and demote() refresh the embedded CertificationRecord.evaluation snapshot instead of leaving level/max_achievable/explanation stale. Added focused inline regressions for both promotion and demotion. Validation: rch check passed; remote targeted cargo test via rch on build env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch_target_bd1wpq cargo test -p frankenengine-node refreshes_embedded_evaluation_snapshot -- --nocapture entered progress-stale state in shared external dependency compilation; local non-cargo sanity checks passed (git diff --check, rustfmt --check on touched file).","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"CloudyAnchor","created_at":"2026-03-11T22:51:26.052117244Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-12T11:28:05.854534439Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"bd-1wpq","title":"Fix certification registry stale evaluation after promote/demote","description":"Fresh-eyes audit found that crates/franken-node/src/supply_chain/certification.rs updates CertificationRecord.level during promote/demote but leaves CertificationRecord.evaluation stale. Downstream readers can observe conflicting certification levels from the same record. Fix the registry to keep evaluation metadata consistent with promoted/demoted state and add focused regressions.","notes":"Patched `crates/franken-node/src/supply_chain/certification.rs` so `CertificationRegistry::promote()` and `demote()` refresh the embedded `CertificationRecord.evaluation` snapshot instead of leaving level/max_achievable/explanation stale. Added focused inline regressions for both promotion and demotion.

Validation history:
- prior `rch` targeted test attempt stalled in shared external dependency compilation without surfacing a source failure
- `rustfmt --edition 2024 --check crates/franken-node/src/supply_chain/certification.rs` PASS
- `git diff --check -- crates/franken-node/src/supply_chain/certification.rs` PASS
- `rch check` PASS
- 2026-03-12 CloudyAnchor retried two isolated remote validation lanes on fresh workers/target dirs:
  - `rch exec -- env TMPDIR=/home/ubuntu CARGO_INCREMENTAL=0 CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch_target_cloudyanchor_bd1wpq RCH_BUILD_TIMEOUT=900 cargo test --manifest-path crates/franken-node/Cargo.toml refreshes_embedded_evaluation_snapshot --lib -- --nocapture`
    - worker `vmi1156319`; progressed through sync + deep dependency compile, then entered recurring `progress_stale` execute state with no source error or green verdict surfaced back to the shell
  - `rch exec -- env TMPDIR=/home/ubuntu CARGO_INCREMENTAL=0 CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch_target_cloudyanchor_bd1wpq_check RCH_BUILD_TIMEOUT=900 cargo check --manifest-path crates/franken-node/Cargo.toml -p frankenengine-node --lib`
    - worker `vmi1153651`; likewise progressed through sync + dependency checking, then entered `progress_stale` execute state with no source error or green verdict surfaced back to the shell
- Both lanes were canceled by CloudyAnchor after reproducing the same infrastructure-stall mode on isolated workers; no local cargo fallback was accepted.

Current state: file-local fix appears landed and stable, but trustworthy remote cargo verification is still infrastructure-blocked, so bead was returned to `open` for the next available closeout pass rather than being over-claimed as verified.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-03-11T22:51:26.052117244Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-12T11:38:59.171194491Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-1wz","title":"[PLAN 10.17] Radical Expansion Execution Track (9K)","description":"Section: 10.17 — Radical Expansion Execution Track (9K)

Strategic Context:
Radical expansion track for proof-carrying speculation, Bayesian adversary control, time-travel replay, ZK attestations, and claim compiler systems.

Execution Requirements:
- Preserve all scoped capabilities from the canonical plan.
- Keep contracts self-contained so future contributors can execute without reopening the master plan.
- Require explicit testing strategy (unit + integration/e2e) and structured logging/telemetry for every child bead.
- Require artifact-backed validation for performance, security, and correctness claims.

Dependency Semantics:
- This epic is blocked by its child implementation beads.
- Cross-epic dependencies encode strategic sequencing and canonical ownership boundaries.

## Success Criteria
- All child beads for this section are completed with acceptance artifacts attached and no unresolved blockers.
- Section-level verification gate for comprehensive unit tests, integration/e2e workflows, and detailed structured logging evidence is green.
- Scope-to-plan traceability is explicit, with no feature loss versus the canonical plan section.

## Optimization Notes
- User-Outcome Lens: "[PLAN 10.17] Radical Expansion Execution Track (9K)" must improve operator confidence, safety posture, and deterministic recovery behavior under both normal and adversarial conditions.
- Cross-Section Coordination: child beads must encode integration assumptions explicitly to avoid local optimizations that degrade system-wide correctness.
- Verification Non-Negotiable: completion requires reproducible unit/integration/E2E evidence and structured logs suitable for independent replay.
- Scope Protection: any simplification must preserve canonical-plan feature intent and be justified with explicit tradeoff documentation.","notes":"Acceptance Criteria alias (plan-space normalization, 2026-02-20):
- The `Success Criteria` section in this bead is the canonical acceptance contract for closure.

 {"id":"bd-1xao","title":"[13] Success criterion: impossible-by-default adoption","description":"Master Plan Source: PLAN_TO_CREATE_FRANKEN_NODE.md
Section: 13

Task Objective:
Track and gate production-grade adoption of impossible-by-default capabilities.

Execution Requirements:
- Make this requirement machine-verifiable and release-gated where applicable.
- Keep behavior self-documenting so future contributors do not need to re-open the master plan.

Testing & Logging Requirements:
- Unit tests for rule/contract logic and error conditions.
- Integration/E2E tests for workflow-level enforcement.
- Structured logs with stable codes and trace IDs.
- Reproducible artifacts that prove contract satisfaction.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Success and failure invariants for [13] Success criterion: impossible-by-default adoption are explicitly quantified and machine-verifiable.
- Determinism requirements for [13] Success criterion: impossible-by-default adoption are validated across repeated runs and adversarial perturbations.


Expected Artifacts:
- Machine-readable verification artifact with explicit canonical path under artifacts/section_13/bd-1xao/verification_evidence.json, suitable for CI/release gating.
- Human-readable verification summary with explicit canonical path under artifacts/section_13/bd-1xao/verification_summary.md, linking implementation intent to measured validation outcomes.

Task-Specific Clarification:
- The implementation must deliver the exact capability named in the title, with no scope dilution and no silent feature omission.
- Validation artifacts must include capability-specific thresholds, pass/fail criteria, and reproducible evidence bundles tied to this bead ID.
- Program/section verification gates must be able to consume this bead's outputs without manual interpretation.

Why This Improves User Outcomes:
- "[13] Success criterion: impossible-by-default adoption" is required to reduce operator error, increase deterministic safety guarantees, and improve time-to-diagnosis for failures in real-world workflows.
- Explicit success/failure evidence for "[13] Success criterion: impossible-by-default adoption" prevents false confidence and improves trust in migration/compatibility/security outcomes.
- Detailed unit + integration/E2E + structured logging requirements ensure reproducible quality and faster incident remediation for users.","acceptance_criteria":"1. 'Impossible-by-default' capabilities are defined: operations that are blocked unless explicitly enabled with cryptographic authorization.
2. At minimum, these capabilities are impossible-by-default: (a) arbitrary file system access outside project root, (b) outbound network to non-allowlisted hosts, (c) spawning child processes without sandbox, (d) loading unsigned extensions, (e) disabling hardening profiles.
3. Each impossible-by-default capability requires explicit opt-in via signed capability token with expiry.
4. Attempting a blocked operation produces a clear, actionable error message (not a generic permission denied).
5. Adoption metric: >= 90% of production deployments run with all impossible-by-default capabilities enforced (measured via telemetry).
6. No impossible-by-default capability can be silently disabled; any disabling is logged and alerted.
7. Evidence: capability_enforcement_report.json listing each capability, enforcement status, and opt-in rate.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"CrimsonCrane","created_at":"2026-02-20T07:39:34.668279352Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-20T23:37:02.590071518Z","closed_at":"2026-02-20T23:37:02.590043385Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["plan","section-13","self-contained","test-obligations"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1xao","depends_on_id":"bd-pga7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-20T07:43:25.443838167Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-1xbc","title":"[10.17] Add deterministic time-travel runtime capture/replay for extension-host workflows.","description":"Master Plan Source: PLAN_TO_CREATE_FRANKEN_NODE.md
Section: 10.17 — Radical Expansion Execution Track (9K)

Why This Exists:
Radical expansion track for proof-carrying speculation, Bayesian adversary control, time-travel replay, ZK attestations, and claim compiler systems.

Task Objective:
Add deterministic time-travel runtime capture/replay for extension-host workflows.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Captured executions replay byte-for-byte equivalent control decisions under same seed/input; incident replay includes stepwise state navigation and divergence explanation.

Expected Artifacts:
- `docs/specs/time_travel_runtime.md`, `src/replay/time_travel_engine.rs`, `tests/lab/time_travel_replay_equivalence.rs`, `artifacts/10.17/time_travel_replay_report.json`.

- Machine-readable verification artifact at `artifacts/section_10_17/bd-1xbc/verification_evidence.json` for CI/release gating.
- Human-readable verification summary at `artifacts/section_10_17/bd-1xbc/verification_summary.md` linking implementation intent to measured outcomes.
Testing & Logging Requirements:
- Unit tests for core logic, invariants, and error handling.
- Integration/E2E tests for end-to-end control-flow behavior.
- Detailed structured logs/telemetry with stable codes and trace correlation IDs.
- Deterministic replay fixture or reproducible artifact for failure analysis.

Task-Specific Clarification:
- For "[10.17] Add deterministic time-travel runtime capture/replay for extension-host workflows.", preserve full capability scope from the plan; no feature compression or silent omission is allowed.
- Verification for "[10.17] Add deterministic time-travel runtime capture/replay for extension-host workflows." must include capability-specific thresholds and machine-readable pass/fail evidence artifacts.
- Unit tests, integration/E2E scripts, and structured logs for "[10.17] Add deterministic time-travel runtime capture/replay for extension-host workflows." must support deterministic replay and root-cause triage without hidden context.

Why This Improves User Outcomes:
- "[10.17] Add deterministic time-travel runtime capture/replay for extension-host workflows." is required to reduce operator error, increase deterministic safety guarantees, and improve time-to-diagnosis for failures in real-world workflows.
- Explicit success/failure evidence for "[10.17] Add deterministic time-travel runtime capture/replay for extension-host workflows." prevents false confidence and improves trust in migration/compatibility/security outcomes.
- Detailed unit + integration/E2E + structured logging requirements ensure reproducible quality and faster incident remediation for users.","acceptance_criteria":"- Captured executions replay byte-for-byte equivalent control decisions under same seed/input; incident replay includes stepwise state navigation and divergence explanation.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"CrimsonCrane","created_at":"2026-02-20T07:37:03.099102362Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-22T05:29:57.447111352Z","closed_at":"2026-02-22T05:29:57.447089121Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["plan","section-10-17","self-contained","test-obligations"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1xbc","depends_on_id":"bd-274s","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-20T07:43:18.349690460Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"bd-1z5a.11","title":"[bd-1z5a] Add signed replay-capsule verification to verifier economy","description":"Why this exists:
`crates/franken-node/src/verifier_economy/mod.rs` currently recomputes replay capsule integrity hashes, freshness, and trace commitments, but the `ReplayCapsule` artifact itself still carries no detached verifier signature or signer key binding. That means a capsule can be internally self-consistent yet still be forgeable by any actor who can populate matching fields.

What this task must do:
- add signed replay-capsule payload binding for the verifier-economy capsule artifact,
- verify that signature during registration/access,
- keep the implementation shared with connector verifier helpers rather than inventing a one-off local signature path,
- add focused inline regression tests for tampered signature / tampered payload / wrong signer cases,
- route cargo-heavy validation through rch only if attempted.

Acceptance criteria:
1. Replay capsules in the verifier economy carry verifier key/signature material and are rejected if the signature does not validate over the canonical capsule payload.
2. Tampering with bound capsule fields after signing is rejected even if the integrity hash is recomputed.
3. The signature helper logic lives in shared verifier code, not duplicated ad hoc in verifier_economy.
4. Focused tests cover success, tampered payload, and wrong signer key.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"PinkPond","created_at":"2026-03-11T04:32:21.012877953Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-11T09:49:34.603358056Z","closed_at":"2026-03-11T09:49:34.603323602Z","close_reason":"Closeout audit 2026-03-11: acceptance criteria satisfied by current tree. crates/franken-node/src/verifier_economy/mod.rs now binds ReplayCapsule signatures to canonical replay_capsule_signature_payload(...) and verifies through cached Ed25519 key checks, with focused regression tests covering tampered signatures, forged claim hash binding, and wrong verifier key. Supporting checker/test evidence: scripts/check_verifier_economy.py --json PASS 152/152 and targeted unittest suites 88 tests OK.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["capsule","replay","security","verifier"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1z5a.11","depends_on_id":"bd-1z5a","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-11T04:32:21.012877953Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-1z5a.12","title":"bd-1z5a support: replace universal verifier SDK hash-as-signature capsules with real Ed25519 binding","description":"Fresh-eye support shard under bd-1z5a. In crates/franken-node/src/connector/universal_verifier_sdk.rs the advertised signed replay capsule path currently computes signature = SHA-256(signing payload) and verifies by recomputing the same hash, which is a trust surrogate rather than detached signature verification. Scope: harden the universal verifier SDK capsule path to use real Ed25519 signature verification with canonical payload binding, add focused inline regressions, keep scope to this file unless a directly required helper import forces a small adjacent touch, and route cargo-heavy validation through rch.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"OrangePond","created_at":"2026-03-11T04:33:12.923572486Z","created_by":"OrangePond","updated_at":"2026-03-11T09:49:43.384573432Z","closed_at":"2026-03-11T09:49:43.384548456Z","close_reason":"Closeout audit 2026-03-11: acceptance criteria satisfied by current tree. crates/franken-node/src/connector/universal_verifier_sdk.rs now uses detached Ed25519 signing and verification via sign_capsule(...) and verify_capsule_signature(...), and focused regressions reject manifest tamper and hash-surrogate signatures.  {"id":"bd-1z5a.15","title":"bd-1z5a support: backfill real rch build IDs into operator E2E artifacts","description":"Why this exists:
The bd-1z5a operator E2E bundle currently records stage commands, exit codes, and event-family evidence, but the machine-readable build provenance is still weak: operator_e2e_bundle.json and operator_e2e_log.jsonl carry null build_id values, and the operator checker only requires summary.build_ids to be a list instead of proving real offloaded build ids were captured. That leaves a truthful functional bundle but an avoidable provenance gap for acceptance criteria 5, 6, and 8.

What this task must do:
- recover the exact rch build ids for the existing successful operator E2E stage commands where possible,
- update the operator harness/checker/tests/artifacts so the bundle fails closed if build provenance silently drops back to null on stages that came from rch lanes,
- keep scope on the operator harness/checker/artifact files and stay off the core verifier Rust files plus NavyRiver's replacement-gap evidence-pack lane.

Acceptance criteria:
1. operator_e2e_bundle.json and operator_e2e_log.jsonl carry concrete build ids for the rch-backed stage entries instead of all-null provenance.
2. operator_e2e_summary.json / .md surface those recovered build ids consistently.
3. scripts/check_verifier_replay_operator_e2e.py and its unit tests fail closed if the required stage build provenance disappears again.
4. Scope stays on tests/e2e, scripts/tests, and operator E2E artifacts only.","notes":"Prepared support lane: telemetry provenance rows recovered and posted in bd-1z5a thread; no edits landed because NavyRiver holds wildcard reservation on operator_e2e artifact files.
